name: lexoid
documentation:
# Lexoid

Lexoid is an efficient document parsing library that supports both LLM-based and non-LLM-based (static) PDF document parsing.

## Motivation:
- Use the multi-modal advancement of LLMs
- Enable convenience for users while driving innovation
- Collaborate with a permissive license

## Installation
To install dependencies:
```
make install
```
or, to install with dev-dependencies:
```
make dev
```

To activate virtual environment:
```
source .venv/bin/activate
```

To use LLM-based parsing, define the following environment variables or create a `.env` file with the following definitions
```
OPENAI_API_KEY=""
GOOGLE_API_KEY=""
```

To build a `.whl` file for testing:
```
poetry build
```

Optionally, to use `Playwright` for retrieving web content with the `.whl` package (else regular requests will be used by default):
```
playwright install --with-deps --only-shell chromium
```

## Usage
[Example Notebook](https://github.com/oidlabs-com/Lexoid/blob/main/examples/example_notebook.ipynb)

Here's a quick example to parse documents using Lexoid:
``` python
from lexoid.api import parse
from lexoid.api import ParserType

parsed_md = parse("https://www.justice.gov/eoir/immigration-law-advisor", parser_type="LLM_PARSE", raw=True)
# or
pdf_path = "path/to/immigration-law-advisor.pdf"
parsed_md = parse(pdf_path, parser_type="LLM_PARSE", raw=True)

print(parsed_md)
```

### Parameters
- path (str): The file path or URL.
- parser_type (str, optional): The type of parser to use ("LLM_PARSE" or "STATIC_PARSE"). Defaults to "AUTO".
- raw (bool, optional): Whether to return raw text or structured data. Defaults to False.
- pages_per_split (int, optional): Number of pages per split for chunking. Defaults to 4.
- max_threads (int, optional): Maximum number of threads for parallel processing. Defaults to 4.
- **kwargs: Additional arguments for the parser.

## Benchmark
Initial results (_more updates soon_)
| Rank | Model/Framework | Similarity | Time (s) |
|------|-----------|------------|----------|
| 1 | gpt-4o | 0.799 | 21.77|
| 2 | gemini-1.5-pro | 0.742 | 15.77 |
| 3 | gpt-4o-mini | 0.721 | 14.86 |
| 4 | gemini-1.5-flash | 0.702 | 4.56 |

functions.name: lexoid.api.parse_chunk
documentation:
Parses a file using the specified parser type.

Args:
    path (str): The file path or URL.
    parser_type (ParserType): The type of parser to use (LLM_PARSE, STATIC_PARSE, or AUTO).
    raw (bool): Whether to return raw text or structured data.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments for the parser.

Returns:
    List[Dict] | str: Parsed document data as a list of dictionaries or raw text.

functions.name: lexoid.api.parse_chunk_list
documentation:
Parses a list of files using the specified parser type.

Args:
    file_paths (list): List of file paths.
    parser_type (ParserType): The type of parser to use.
    raw (bool): Whether to return raw text or structured data.
    kwargs (dict): Additional arguments for the parser.

Returns:
    List[Dict | str]: List of parsed documents with raw text and/or metadata.

functions.name: lexoid.api.parse
documentation:
Parses a document or URL, optionally splitting it into chunks and using multiprocessing.

Args:
    path (str): The file path or URL.
    parser_type (Union[str, ParserType], optional): The type of parser to use ("LLM_PARSE", "STATIC_PARSE", or "AUTO"). Defaults to "LLM_PARSE".
    raw (bool, optional): Whether to return raw text or structured data. Defaults to False.
    pages_per_split (int, optional): Number of pages per split for chunking. Defaults to 4.
    max_processes (int, optional): Maximum number of processes for parallel processing. Defaults to 4.
    **kwargs: Additional arguments for the parser.

Returns:
    Union[List[Dict], str]: Parsed document data as a list of dictionaries or raw text.

modules.name: lexoid.core.prompt_templates
documentation: Initial prompt,
This might go through further changes as the library evolves.

functions.name: lexoid.core.parse_type.llm_parser.convert_pdf_page_to_base64
documentation: Convert a PDF page to a base64-encoded PNG string.

functions.name: lexoid.core.parse_type.static_parser.process_table
documentation: Convert a table to markdown format.

functions.name: lexoid.core.parse_type.static_parser.embed_links_in_text
documentation:
Embed hyperlinks inline within the text, matching their position based on rectangles.

Args:
    page (pdfplumber.page.Page): The page containing the links.
    text (str): The full text extracted from the page.
    links (list of tuples): List of (rect, uri) pairs.

Returns:
    str: The text with hyperlinks embedded inline.

functions.name: lexoid.core.parse_type.static_parser.process_pdf_page_with_pdfplumber
documentation: Process a single page's content and return formatted markdown text.

functions.name: lexoid.core.parse_type.static_parser.process_pdf_with_pdfplumber
documentation: Process PDF and return a list of markdown-formatted strings, one per page.

functions.name: lexoid.core.parse_type.static_parser.parse_with_pdfplumber
documentation:
Parse PDF and return either raw text or structured data.

Args:
    path (str): Path to the PDF file
    raw (bool): If True, return raw text with page breaks; if False, return structured data
    **kwargs: Additional arguments including 'title' and 'start' page number

Returns:
    Union[List[Dict], str]: Either a list of dictionaries containing page metadata and content,
                           or a string of raw text with page breaks

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.calculate_similarity
documentation: Calculate similarity ratio between two texts using SequenceMatcher.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.convert_pdf_page_to_image
documentation: Convert a PDF page to an image.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.get_file_type
documentation: Get the file type of a file based on its extension.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.is_supported_file_type
documentation: Check if the file type is supported for parsing.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.is_supported_url_file_type
documentation:
Check if the file type from the URL is supported.

Args:
    url (str): The URL of the file.

Returns:
    bool: True if the file type is supported, False otherwise.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.download_file
documentation:
Downloads a file from the given URL and saves it to a temporary directory.

Args:
    url (str): The URL of the file to download.
    temp_dir (str): The temporary directory to save the file.

Returns:
    str: The path to the downloaded file.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.find_dominant_heading_level
documentation:
Finds the most common heading level that occurs more than once.
Also checks for underline style headings (---).

Args:
    markdown_content (str): The markdown content to analyze

Returns:
    str: The dominant heading pattern (e.g., '##' or 'underline')

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.split_md_by_headings
documentation:
Splits markdown content by the specified heading pattern and structures it.

Args:
    url (str): The URL of the HTML page
    markdown_content (str): The markdown content to split
    heading_pattern (str): The heading pattern to split on (e.g., '##' or 'underline')

Returns:
    List[Dict]: List of dictionaries containing metadata and content

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.html_to_markdown
documentation:
Converts HTML content to markdown.

Args:
    html (str): The HTML content to convert.
    raw (bool): Whether to return raw markdown text or structured data.

Returns:
    Union[str, List[Dict]]: Either raw markdown content or structured data with metadata and content sections.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.read_html_content
documentation:
Reads the content of an HTML page from the given URL and converts it to markdown or structured content.

Args:
    url (str): The URL of the HTML page.
    raw (bool): Whether to return raw markdown text or structured data.

Returns:
    Union[str, List[Dict]]: Either raw markdown content or structured data with metadata and content sections.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.extract_urls_from_markdown
documentation:
Extracts URLs from markdown content using regex.
Matches both [text](url) and bare http(s):// URLs.

Args:
    content (str): Markdown content to search for URLs

Returns:
    List[str]: List of unique URLs found

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.recursive_read_html
documentation:
Recursively reads HTML content from URLs up to specified depth.

Args:
    url (str): The URL to parse
    depth (int): How many levels deep to recursively parse
    raw (bool): Whether to return raw text or structured data
    visited_urls (set): Set of already visited URLs to prevent cycles

Returns:
    Union[str, List[Dict]]: Combined content from all parsed URLs

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.save_webpage_as_pdf
documentation:
Saves a webpage as a PDF file using PyQt5.

Args:
    url (str): The URL of the webpage.
    output_path (str): The path to save the PDF file.

Returns:
    str: The path to the saved PDF file.

functions.name: lexoid.core.utils.convert_to_pdf
documentation:
Converts a file or webpage to PDF.

Args:
    input_path (str): The path to the input file or URL.
    output_path (str): The path to save the output PDF file.

Returns:
    str: The path to the saved PDF file.
